description_long: This object is a solar energy infrastructure installation featuring several rectangular solar panels mounted on a flat, rectangular base. The panels are arranged in a linear row, each panel supported by angled brackets that elevate them above the base and provide a fixed tilt. The base appears to be constructed from a light-colored material, possibly concrete or composite, with additional structural elements and mounting hardware visible. The solar panels themselves are dark, likely glass-covered photovoltaic modules, framed with metallic edges. There are no visible electrical inverters or cabling on the exterior, and the mounting structure is robust, suggesting a ground-mounted or flat-roof installation. The overall design is compact and modular, suitable for urban environments such as rooftops, parking lots, or small utility installations.
description: A compact solar energy installation with several dark, glass-covered panels mounted on a light rectangular base, supported by angled brackets and metallic frames.
description_view_0: From this view, the solar panels are seen head-on, showing their rectangular shape and the supporting brackets beneath them.
description_view_1: This side view highlights the tilt of the solar panels and the structural supports connecting them to the base.
description_view_2: The rear view reveals the back of the panels and the mounting hardware, with the base structure clearly visible.
description_view_3: This opposite side view again shows the panel tilt and the arrangement of the panels along the base.
attribute_panel_orientation: tilted at approximately 30 degrees, azimuth direction not clearly discernible, orientation appears fixed
attribute_mounting_structure_type: ground-mounted or flat-roof mounted with metallic (likely aluminum) angled brackets and a rectangular base
attribute_array_configuration: linear row of 3 solar panels mounted side by side
